_sources.fact: Hickory has 189 furniture manufacturers employing over 14,000 people in the metro area, stemming from 1902 when local businessmen decided to build furniture from regional lumber supplies.
Hickory Furniture Mart was established in 1960 and opened to the public in 1985, becoming one of North Carolina's top visitor attractions with nearly 1 million square feet of showroom space.
CommScope, which was founded in Hickory, invested $60.3 million in 2023 to expand fiber-optic cable manufacturing operations, creating 250+ new jobs for rural broadband deployment.

metaDesc: Custom web design and SEO for Hickory and Catawba County businesses: furniture makers, manufacturers, contractors, and shops. Sites you own, no templates.

schemaDesc: Web development and design in Hickory, NC. Custom websites, SEO, and branding for furniture manufacturers, contractors, restaurants, and small businesses in Catawba County, Conover, Newton, and Maiden.
heroSub: Custom websites, SEO, and branding for the furniture makers, manufacturers, contractors, and shops of Hickory and the Catawba Valley. No templates, no lock-in, and you own every line of it.
localH2: Built for Hickory and the Catawba Valley.
localP1: Hickory's identity is built on furniture and manufacturing. In 1902, local businessmen decided this region's abundant lumber should make furniture right here, and today the Hickory metro is home to 189 furniture manufacturers employing over 14,000 people. The Hickory Furniture Mart draws visitors nationwide, while CommScope, founded here, continues to anchor advanced manufacturing, recently investing in fiber-optic cable production for rural broadband.
localP2: We build for the people who power this economy: the furniture maker and the factory floor, the contractor and the cable technician, the restaurant downtown, the retail and service businesses that keep the Catawba Valley moving. Every site is designed and coded around your business, not stamped out of a template.
mapMetaLine: Serving Hickory, Conover, Newton, Maiden, Morganton & the Catawba Valley

faqOutsideAnswer: Yes. We serve Catawba County, Conover, Newton, Maiden, Morganton, and throughout North Carolina, all done remotely with recorded walkthroughs.
